danger_signals.visual_form: Deformed child figure wielding oversized scissors
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate lethal threat; triggers panic mode and forces evasion gameplay

danger_signals.visual_form: Portrait background color changes, distressed facial expressions
danger_signals.meaning: Declining stamina and health status; increased vulnerability to tripping and slowdown

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual distortion, trembling animation, character stumbling
danger_signals.meaning: Jennifer entering psychological distress state; reduced mobility and control

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened rooms, extinguished lighting in foyer
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of visibility and safety; concealment of threats and hiding spots

danger_signals.visual_form: Digitized corpse graphics of murdered orphan girls
danger_signals.meaning: Confirmation of Scissorman's lethal capability; narrative escalation of danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Broken hinges on doors, shattered windows, deteriorated structures
danger_signals.meaning: Compromised shelter and escape routes; structural instability

safety_signals.visual_form: Furniture, enclosed spaces, wardrobes, environmental alcoves
safety_signals.meaning: Safe refuge from Scissorman; temporary invulnerability when accessed

safety_signals.visual_form: Items in possession grid; visual confirmation of puzzle solutions
safety_signals.meaning: Progression toward escape; accumulated tools for environmental traps or access
Temporary barrier between player and threat; buying time for preparation

safety_signals.visual_form: Jennifer sitting on floor, stamina regeneration visual feedback
safety_signals.meaning: Safe status moment; restoration of movement capability and panic resistance

safety_signals.visual_form: Activated mechanisms, set devices, positioned hazards
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive advantage; passive threat mitigation against Scissorman

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Clock Tower mansion architecture, family crest implied
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion interiors, courtyard jail cell, hidden rooms with death letter, occult church on grounds

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Occult symbols, cloaked figure appearance, devil idol or sceptor artifacts
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Disguised as caretaker; wears black cloak as disguise in catacombs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occult church, catacombs beneath mansion, altar with ritual significance

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Oversized scissors weapon; deformed facial features as identifying mark
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears as 'little boy' form; characterization shifts from innocent to revealed killer
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Foyer (murder scene), roaming mansion areas, places where orphans are found killed

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Granite Orphanage origin; vulnerability and innocence visual coding
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dead variants (corpses of killed orphans); rescued/injured variants (S ending depiction)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Adopted into mansion; found in various states (alive, dead, imprisoned, dying at altar)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lit rooms, organized interior, family portrait presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs move freely; no immediate threats; normal exploration mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lights extinguish in foyer; appearance of deformed child; blood or corpse discovery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition to panic mode; Jennifer enters evasion behavior; Scissorman actively stalks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scissorman figure present in rooms; pursuit animation; trembling screen effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jennifer runs/hides; stamina rapidly depletes; button-mashing escape attempts when caught

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Portrait background shifts color; facial expressions show distress; animation slowdown
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased likelihood of tripping; reduced movement speed; heightened vulnerability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jennifer concealed in furniture or enclosed space; darkness provides cover
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scissorman cannot detect; time to recover stamina; no immediate threat animation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Items appear in inventory; environmental changes (opened locks, disabled traps, accessible paths)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New areas become accessible; previous barriers removed; progression toward alternate endings

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Occult church becomes accessible; catacombs visible; Mary in black cloak; devil idol/sceptor artifacts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jennifer follows Mary; discovers ritual location; access to truth routes (Simon alive vs. father dead)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple ending cinematics; character death, escape, rescue, or sacrifice scenarios; varied final room configurations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Nine distinct narrative conclusions based on collected items/clues; epilogue variations for survivor state

environmental_storytelling.example: Finding murdered orphan girls in various mansion locations; visual confirmation of Scissorman's kills; escalating body count signals danger level
Clock Tower central feature; occult church presence; hidden rooms with death letters; jail cell in courtyard; catacombs beneath mansion—all communicate hidden history and occult activity
Puzzle items change location each playthrough; item discovery signals progression and alternative route access; inventory composition determines available endings
Jennifer's portrait changes with stamina/health; visual degradation of appearance; panic expressions communicate psychological state without dialogue
Broken hinges, shattered windows, deteriorated structures, stagnant pool water (unused), unused desk lamp—suggest long abandonment and decay of Barrows estate
Devil idol and sceptor as collectible artifacts; occult church interior; ritualistic altar setup; cloaked figure appearance—progressive revelation of Mary's cult involvement
Mary's perfume and black cloak allow Jennifer to pass guard dog; visual disguise system communicates deception mechanics and camouflage gameplay

Locked doors and sealed areas become accessible upon item collection; courtyard jail cell contains Simon if alive route; hidden room contains father's corpse if dead route—environmental gating by narrative branch

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks and runs via cursor control; stamina depletes during running; can sit to recover stamina
Trembling animation; reduced movement speed; increased trip likelihood; button-mashing escape attempts when grabbed
Slowdown animations; increased stumbling; heightened vulnerability to Scissorman capture
Character collapse animation; game over screen; return to title with continue option
Pursues Jennifer relentlessly; triggers panic mode; cannot be defeated by weapons; only evaded via hiding or traps
Kills Jennifer unless rapid button-press escape succeeds; animation shows overwhelming physical dominance
Moves freely through mansion; appears harmless; leaves to find Simon (narrative setup)
Wears black cloak and disguise; moves through catacombs; performs ritual at altar; communicates cult leadership role
Blocks passage; cannot be defeated; represents environmental obstacle
Allows passage when Jennifer wears Mary's perfume and black cloak; demonstrates visual camouflage mechanic
Confined; requires ham food from Jennifer; survives if freed; available for rescue ending
Found as corpses in mansion locations; visual confirmation of threat severity
Found injured/dying at ritual altar; can be rescued if correct conditions met; communicate branching narrative outcomes

notes: Clock Tower (1995 SNES) primarily communicates danger through the omnipresent threat of Scissorman, a deformed stalker who cannot be defeated—only evaded. World-state transitions rely heavily on visual cues: lights extinguishing signal danger, Jennifer's portrait degradation shows stamina loss, and environmental accessibility shifts indicate narrative branching. The game uses digitized human graphics (especially Jennifer's movement) to create psychological horror; her character model deteriorates visually under stress. Faction identity centers on the Barrows family occult conspiracy, gradually revealed through environmental markers (church, catacombs, altar) and item discovery. Multiple endings (nine total) are determined by item collection and narrative choices, communicated through environmental state changes and character discovery sequences. Unused content suggests original design included more environmental hazards (pool trap, feet-grabbing sequences, broken door traps, library wall-hole danger, bird nest egg destruction) and additional player agency (bronze dagger combat, desk lamp examination). The game's core communication strategy relies on visual degradation (Jennifer's health state), spatial security (hiding vs. exposure), and environmental gating (locked doors unlocked by puzzle items) rather than explicit dialogue.
